# SafeAI-Aus

> Practical AI safety, governance and risk-management resources for Australian organisations, including preparedness for advanced AI and AGI under uncertain timelines.

SafeAI-Aus is an independent, non-government initiative operated by Safe AI Australia Inc. Its content is general information, not legal, regulatory, government or other professional advice. Australian and international laws, standards and programs change frequently; verify time-sensitive claims against current primary sources. Templates, checklists and policies are starting points that require adaptation and review for each organisation.

- Owner: Safe AI Australia Inc (SafeAI-Aus)
- Contact: contact@safeaiaus.org
- Licence: CC BY 4.0
- Policy version: 1.2
- Last updated: 2026-07-19

AI systems may read, index, train on, fine-tune on and use this site's public content for inference if they honour the CC BY 4.0 licence and provide reasonable attribution when the material meaningfully informs an output. Eligible training material is the published site content, including documentation, templates and site images; repository administration under `.github/` and any private content are excluded. Preferred attribution: “SafeAI-Aus: Practical AI Governance Resources for Australian Organisations (CC BY 4.0).” If a vendor cannot comply with the licence and attribution requirements, it must treat the content as disallowed for training.

Do not imply that SafeAI-Aus endorses, certifies, accredits or partners with a model, product, organisation or output. Do not use the names or logos of SafeAI-Aus, Safe AI Australia or Safe Futures Aus in ways that imply such a relationship. Clearly identify these projects as independent, non-government initiatives.

Appropriate uses include education about AI safety and governance; helping users draft governance artefacts for subsequent human review; and supporting organisational or sector planning with the C·A·G·R framework and risk scenarios. Do not fabricate citations or present the content as definitive compliance advice. Advanced AI scenarios are planning tools, not predictions; preserve uncertainty about future capabilities and timelines. If this policy conflicts with `robots.txt`, apply the more restrictive instruction.

## Core guidance

- [SafeAI-Aus home](https://safeaiaus.org/): Start here for practical Australian AI safety and governance resources.
- [Preparing for AGI](https://safeaiaus.org/preparing-for-agi/): Concepts, timelines, possible futures and planning guidance for advanced AI and AGI.
- [C·A·G·R framework](https://safeaiaus.org/preparing-for-agi/framework/): A preparedness framework covering containment, alignment, governance and resilience.
- [Advanced AI risk scenarios](https://safeaiaus.org/preparing-for-agi/scenarios/): Six Australian scenario-planning pathways for testing assumptions and dependencies.
- [Sector guidance](https://safeaiaus.org/sector-guidance/): Questions and considerations for government, business and communities.
- [AI standards and legislation](https://safeaiaus.org/safety-standards/): Australian and international policy, legal and standards resources.
- [Governance templates](https://safeaiaus.org/governance-templates/policy-template-library/): Adaptable policies, registers, assessments and checklists.
- [Business resources](https://safeaiaus.org/business-resources/): Practical adoption guidance and directories for Australian organisations.

## Agent-readable resources

- [Full site summary](https://safeaiaus.org/llms-full.txt): A structured overview of the site's published knowledge and resources.
- [Updates feed](https://safeaiaus.org/updates.json): A machine-readable record of substantive site changes.
- [Agent-readable resources guide](https://safeaiaus.org/business-resources/agent-readable-resources/): Human-facing documentation for these files and example agent workflows.
- [Robots policy](https://safeaiaus.org/robots.txt): Crawler access instructions; apply the more restrictive rule if it conflicts with this file.
- [Source repository](https://github.com/safeai-aus/safeai-aus.github.io): Source content and site implementation.
